commit_scope: Let the public web health proxy report the live API deployed SHA when local web config does not expose one.

commands: cd web && npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false --incremental false
local_validation.summary: The web health-proxy fallback type-checks and the production Next build passes.

deploy_validation.details: Pending Hostinger auto-deploy and strict public SHA validation after merge.

phase_gate.reason: Local web proof is complete; CI and public deploy proof remain pending.

evidence_refs: After d627983d3c0e46b26829c210656502758c233eab deployed, strict public verification still failed because /api/health-proxy returned web.deployed_sha=unknown.
The same live health-proxy payload included api.deployed_sha=d627983d3c0e46b26829c210656502758c233eab from the upstream API health response.

The Hostinger deployment topology rebuilds API and web together from the same target SHA, so the web proxy now reports the upstream deployed SHA as api:deployed_sha when local web config is unavailable.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: When local web config is unavailable but upstream API health exposes deployed_sha, /api/health-proxy reports web.deployed_sha and web.updated_at from api.deployed_sha with source api:deployed_sha.
test_flows: Strict public deploy verification with VERIFY_REQUIRE_WEB_HEALTH_PROXY_SHA=1
